headline: Disability Services Budget Frozen at RM320 Million for 4th Year
context: JKM allocation for disability services has not increased since 2022 despite growing registration of persons with disabilities.

cards.big: Disability services budget frozen at RM320 million while OKU registrations grew 18% since 2022
cards.sub: Per-person allocation effectively declined from RM520 to RM440 annually

cards.big: Only 612,000 of estimated 1.8 million persons with disabilities are registered with JKM
cards.sub: Two-thirds of eligible individuals miss out on welfare support and assistive devices

cards.big: OKU monthly allowance of RM300 has not changed since 2019 despite 18% cumulative inflation
cards.sub: Real value of disability support continues to erode against cost of living

cards.big: UK Personal Independence Payment averages GBP450 monthly adjusted annually for inflation
cards.sub: Index-linked disability benefits protect recipients from purchasing power erosion

cards.big: Freezing disability funding while costs rise is a silent cut imposed on the most vulnerable
Should disability allowances be automatically indexed to inflation like pension payments
